Design system · living document

Warm paper, quiet ink,
and eight muted feelings

Everything in this document is generated from the shipped app. Every token carries the same name it has in code, so in a design conversation you can point at anything here by name: "make ochre600 colder", "swap the display voice", and the change lands in Theme.swift and ships everywhere at once.

Derived from Theme.swift and Components.swift · July 10, 2026 · toggle the theme to inspect the espresso dark ramp
01

The horizon mark

A hairline sun on a horizon, half risen. Ochre ring and horizon line, tan half-disc below.

Primary mark

Assets.xcassets/logo.horizon · logo-light.svg / logo-dark.svg
Rendered via HorizonMotif (default 118 pt). The asset ships its own dark variant in the assigned dark ochres; it is never template-tinted. Ask before authoring any new cut of the mark.

On the scrim

recording, streak and coach overlays
Overlay surfaces are fixed near-black (overlayScrim) in both themes, so the dark cut of the mark lives there permanently.
02

Color

Warm cream paper, warm-charcoal ink, one caramel accent. Dark mode is a warm espresso ramp, not an inversion. Tap any swatch or hex to copy it.

Paper · surfaces

Ink · text and lines

Ochre · the accent ramp

Functional

Fixed overlay palette · identical in both themes

Semantic aliases · what code actually calls

bgApppaper1
surfaceCardpaper0
surfaceRaisedpaper2
surfaceSunkenpaper3
textHeadingink800
textBodyink700
textMutedink500
textSubtleink300
textOnDarkpaper0
accentochre600
motifFillink900
03

The eight moods

Every feeling the model detects folds into one of eight muted buckets. Each mood also owns a piano chord: all eight are voiced in C major inside a single octave, so any two played together stay consonant.

Tap a mood to hear its chord, played on the same piano sample the app uses (piano_d5.wav, pitched per note).
04

Three voices

Settled: v2 is adopted. One family, Chillax, now carries display, UI and the diary text; weights stop at semibold and the old italic-ochre signature becomes a color-only move. Use the version dropdown to flip between Control (what the app ships today) and v2. The specimen cards below stay pinned to their own faces regardless.

v2 · Chillax everywhere — ADOPTEDone family for display, UI and the diary text · Cormorant, Newsreader and Hanken all retire
Weights drop one notch across the board and Chillax never exceeds semibold: big display sits at light (300 to 350), titles at medium, buttons and labels at regular to medium. Entry text sets in Chillax regular with the same generous leading; the italic-ochre signature becomes a color-only move since Chillax has no italic cut. Flip the dropdown to v2 and judge it on the screens, especially the transcript.
Display · CormorantFonts/Cormorant-VF.ttf · Typography.display(size, weight, italic)
A quiet record of how the days felt
LightRegularMediumSemiBoldBoldItalic
Screen titles, the paywall headline, onboarding moments. The signature move is medium italic in ochre600, used sparingly.
Reading · NewsreaderFonts/Newsreader-VF.ttf · Typography.reading(size, weight, italic)
Walked to the studio in the rain and didn't mind it. The new paywall finally feels like part of the app instead of a gate bolted on, and for the first time in weeks I closed the laptop before midnight.
Entry text, transcripts, reflections. Set at 17 to 19 pt with generous line height; never used for chrome.
Shipping UI · Hanken GroteskFonts/HankenGrotesk-VF.ttf · Typography.ui(size, weight, italic) · what the app uses today
This week in feelings Continue with email Recorded Tuesday · 2 min 40 sec
LightRegularMediumSemiboldBoldItalic
Buttons, labels, metadata, tab bar. Small-caps labels are 11 pt semibold with 1.6 tracking (DSLabel). Retired: kept here only as the before-picture.

Exceptions worth knowing: the Apple sign-in button keeps the system font (Apple requires it), so the Google button beside it matches at SF 19 medium. Numerals that align in columns get monospaced digits.

05

Space and shape

A 4 px rhythm for spacing, one continuous-corner radius scale for everything rounded.

Spacing · Theme.Space

values in points
gutter (18) is the screen-edge padding on every screen.

Radii · Theme.Radius

continuous (squircle) corners everywhere
Cards sit on lg (22). Sheets and the paywall use xl to xxl. pill (999) is every capsule button.
06

Elevation

Shadows are warm brown on paper (#2B2014), pure black on espresso, always cast downward. Three steps only.

sm
opacity .10 · radius 5 · y 3
rows, small chips, pressed FAB
md
opacity .16 · radius 14 · y 8
the default card shadow (dsShadow)
lg
opacity .22 · radius 26 · y 14
tab bar, floating record button
07

Paper grain

Every screen sits on cream paper with a tiled noise texture in Overlay blend (dsPaper). Espresso shows grain far more than cream, so dark mode dials it back.

bare bgApp
dsPaper · light · opacity 0.90
dsPaper · dark · opacity 0.36

The blend is isolated with a compositing group so the grain never tints content, only the paper behind it. Source tile: Assets.xcassets/noise_overlay.

08

Icons

All icons come from the Nucleo sets, never hand-drawn. Line icons (ic.*, tab.*) are template-tinted to any ink or accent color via the Icon component, 18 pt default. Glass icons and life-area discs keep their own colors.

09

Components

The primitives everything is assembled from. Demos are live HTML approximations; the source of truth is the Swift on the right of each caption.

DSCard

Components.swift · paper card, radius lg, hairlineSoft stroke, md shadow
Tuesday evening
Long walk after the studio, everything felt lighter.
With motif
CardMotif: filled quarter-disc (r 40, motifFill) under a hairline arc (r 78), clipped by the corner.

Glass card

DSCard(glass: true) · ultraThinMaterial + paper0 at 45%
Over a mood wash
Glass cards float over colored or photographic ground, with a bright hairline.

Buttons

LoginView, PaywallView · capsules, height 56
Primary is an ink900 capsule with paper0 text, so it flips to cream-on-espresso in dark mode. Selection chips fill with ochre600. Every one of these presses with HapticPressStyle.

Floating record button

HomeView · FloatingButtonStyle
Rests at lg elevation, same as the tab bar. Pressing scales to 0.9 and drops the shadow to sm, so it reads as physically pushed down.

Week strip

WeekStripView · streak flame + six day discs
4
Fri3
Sat4
Sun5
Mon6
Tue7
Wed8
The active day fills with its entry's mood color (caramel when empty). Days with entries are paper0 discs; empty days are dashed hairline over paper2. The leftmost slot is the streak sun with the day count above it.

Mood square

MoodSquare · slice-and-dice treemap, 40 pt, corner 11
Each entry's feeling blend as a tiny treemap: area is proportional to weight, the largest mood splits along the longer axis first. The leading marker for every feed row.

Small-caps label

DSLabel · ui 11 semibold, tracking 1.6, ink300
This week in feelings Reflections
The section voice of the whole app. Color overrides to ochre600 for accent sections.

Callout pill

EntryDetailView HUD · fixed calloutDark + calloutText in both themes
Joy · while this part plays Entry saved
Floating status over content. Deliberately never flips with the theme, so it reads as the same object everywhere.
10

The key screens

Four screens rebuilt from the shipped SwiftUI, wired to the same tokens as everything above. Change a token and these re-skin with it; flip the theme to proof the espresso versions. Sizes are true points at iPhone width.

Home feed · HomeView.swift. Pinned week strip, display greeting with the italic ochre name, day groups as 2-up mood-tinted cards (tint = mood color mixed 18% into paper1), glass tab pill + ink900 record FAB.
9:41
July 8 · Overall calm
Rain on the studio roof
Subjects Transcript Edit
The rain started right as I left the studio, which felt almost scripted.
I keep thinking about how the paywall finally feels like part of the app instead of a gate bolted on.
Dan texted that his whole team wants invites, which never happens.
Tomorrow I want to clean up the dark colors before the next build.
Calm
Entry detail · EntryDetailView.swift, read-along state. Cormorant title with mood eyebrow, playing audio ring, Subjects/Transcript underline tabs, per-sentence mood rules; the spoken sentence carries the current words at full ink while everything else sits at 50%. HUD pill + scrubbing dots pinned under a paper fade.

Did the rain walk help clear your head?
2:41

RECORDING
Recording overlay · RecordingOverlay.swift. The scrim is the live mood color mixed 82% into overlayScrim and retints as you talk; follow-up cards in cream glass, decorative equalizer, live feeling pills. The top warning pill is the [NEEDS DESIGN] surface.

The quotes that make up your day to day

Reflections · InsightsView.swift. Display title with Newsreader subtitle, six centered life-area cards (three shown) tinted by each area's mood, category chips on paper0 capsules with mood dots, ochre text button to the full categories screen.
11

Motion, touch and sound

One spring for everything: response 0.28, damping 0.6. Every button gives touch feedback, and most give a light haptic on press-down.

StylePress behaviorUsed on
HapticPressStylescale 0.96 + opacity 0.85 + light impact hapticthe default for every button; scale eases toward 0.99 for large surfaces
PressScaleStylescale 0.93, no haptic in the styleentry tiles, timeline pills
CandlePressStylescale 0.86 + opacity 0.55the streak sun
FloatingButtonStylescale 0.9 + shadow morphs lg to smthe record FAB

Sound: each mood strikes a three-note chord from one key (C major) inside one octave (D4 to E5), sampled from a single piano note and pitched per chord tone. No tritones, no diminished chords, so scrubbing the timeline glides instead of jarring. Playback keeps the screen awake; mood changes during playback stay silent.

12

Where it lives

Change the token, and every screen follows. Nothing here is defined twice.

Theme.swiftevery color, mood, font lookup, spacing, radius and shadow token
Components.swiftDSLabel, DSCard, CardMotif, HorizonMotif, WeekStripView, MoodSquare, Icon, all press styles
Grain.swiftdsPaper, the paper + noise background
Haptics.swiftimpact helpers behind the press styles
MoodAudio.swiftthe piano sampler and mood chords
Fonts/Cormorant, Newsreader, Hanken Grotesk variable fonts (+ italics)
Assets.xcassetslogo.horizon, streak.sun, noise_overlay, ic.* / tab.* / area.* icon sets
design/design-system.htmlthis document · regenerate with design/build-design-system.py
13

The de-Claude pass

On the launch checklist since July 10: make the app look less AI-default. This is the map of where that look actually lives, so the pass is surgical instead of a repaint. You call the direction; fill in a line and I execute it system-wide.

1 · The beige is the paper ramp

Warm cream ground + elegant serif + caramel accent is the single most recognizable AI-default combination, and dyeary currently wears all three. The beige specifically lives in seven tokens: paper0 through canvas, plus ochre100/200 and both hairlines. Swap those and the whole app follows; the espresso dark ramp would need a matching rethink.

Your call: new ground direction, or keep paper but shift its temperature?

2 · The accent is caramel

ochre600 sits squarely in the terracotta/caramel family every AI mock reaches for. One accent hue change re-colors buttons, links, the selected states, the logo ring and the display italic moments at once.

Your call: a colder or stranger accent, or own the caramel and differentiate elsewhere?

3 · The display face carries the look

Cormorant medium italic in ochre is the most "AI serif" move in the system. Newsreader and Hanken Grotesk read as chosen; the display voice is where a new face would change the personality fastest, and it only touches one lookup table in Theme.swift.

Your call: keep, re-weight (less italic, tighter sizes), or audition replacements?

4 · What already reads as ours

The eight-mood palette, the horizon mark, the paper grain, the chord sound system, the treemap mood squares and the week strip are distinctive and worth protecting through the pass. Whatever changes, these are the things a tester would miss.

Your call: confirm the keep list, or nominate casualties.
dyeary design system · generated from the app source on July 10, 2026 · repo: design/design-system.html · tokens: dyeary/dyeary/Theme.swift
